oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Aggregate sampling to detect pathogens and antimicrobial resistance genes associated with bovine respiratory disease in U.S. feedlots: A pilot study
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Jobman, Erin
Vander Ley, Brian
Loy, John Dustin
Loy, Duan Sriyotee
Meyer, Nathan
Thomson, Dan
Lowe, James
Terrell, Shane
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Bovine respiratory disease (BRD) is the leading cause of feedlot morbidity and mortality. Field diagnosis is often limited to visu­al examination, as available diagnostics reflect individual ani­mals only and require labor, animal restraint and time. Aggre­gate sampling techniques are valuable tools in other species but are lacking in the beef industry. This pilot study investigates the plausibility of using the water tank as an aggregate sample substrate in pens of confined cattle.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 258
